era_context: Identical to -2354, -2353, and -2352: Old Kingdom Egypt in terminal decline, Akkadian Empire at zenith under Naram-Sin, Indus Valley Civilization in mature phase, Early Bronze Age III in the Levant and Anatolia.

events.title: Egyptian Old Kingdom: final phase of Sixth Dynasty; regional fragmentation
events.description: The Sixth Dynasty approaches its end. Central authority is severely weakened. Nomarchs in Upper Egypt operate as quasi-independent rulers. Lower Egypt may be controlled by rival factions. The pharaoh's ability to command resources and labor is minimal. Monumental building ceases. The transition to the First Intermediate Period is imminent. Some scholars place the formal end of the Sixth Dynasty around this time or within the next few years.
key_figures: Pepi II (if still reigning) or his successor
events.certainty_note: The exact year of the Sixth Dynasty's end is uncertain, with estimates ranging from c. 2181 to 2160 BCE. The year -2351 is assigned to this period by convention.

events.title: Akkadian Empire: zenith of territorial control; internal strains mounting
events.description: Naram-Sin's empire reaches its maximum territorial extent. However, rebellions in conquered territories increase in frequency and intensity. The Gutians, a highland people from the Zagros, begin to pose a serious threat to Akkadian control. Administrative costs of maintaining such a vast empire strain resources. Military campaigns become more frequent and costly. The empire remains powerful, but the seeds of its collapse are being sown.
events.certainty_note: Naram-Sin's reign is well-documented, but specific year-to-year events are not always precisely dated.

events.title: Indus Valley Civilization: mature phase continues; no evidence of disruption
events.description: The mature Harappan civilization continues to flourish without apparent disruption. Urban centers maintain their standardized planning and infrastructure. Trade networks remain active. Craft production continues at established standards. The civilization shows remarkable stability and continuity. No evidence of major political upheaval, warfare, or environmental crisis is apparent during this period.
events.certainty_note: The mature Harappan phase is archaeologically well-established. Year-to-year variation is not distinguishable from the archaeological record.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence for -2351 identified. The patterns described are consistent with the broader historical trajectory of this period.
historiographic_note: The year -2351 remains undocumented in specific terms. The entries describe the general historical context and trends. Ancient chronologies for the third millennium BCE are uncertain to within ±5–10 years, and year-to-year documentation is rare. The historical record for this year is essentially identical to -2354, -2353, and -2352.
